L1.area: Tensor computation kernel layer for mathematical operations

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists where a tensor operation crashes with a segmentation fault when given input tensors with zero-sized dimensions, causing a denial of service.

L3.area: Dot product computation kernel implementations for CPU and GPU backends
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the dot product tensor operation where passing input tensors with zero-sized dimensions (e.g., shape [0, 0]) causes a segmentation fault and runtime crash due to missing validation of tensor element count before accessing tensor data.

ground_truth.reason: The dot product kernel dereferences tensor data pointers without first checking whether the output tensor has zero elements, causing a segmentation fault when inputs have zero-sized dimensions.
